commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=f04e734712b61e97174e11f72355fa888f99614c)
commands.review_summary:
The PR changes workspace bootstrap file reads, extra-bootstrap glob expansion, bootstrap hook dispatch, embedded-runner timing instrumentation, and related tests to reduce event-loop blocking during embedded_run bootstrap-context.

PR surface: Source +159, Tests +428. Total +587 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live embedded_run reproduction was run in this read-only review. The linked issue has production liveness logs, and current-main source confirms the implicated sync read, fs.glob expansion, and sequential hook path inside bootstrap-context.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Two narrow mechanical repairs are safe for an automated fix lane: replace the undefined test calls and preserve unmatched-glob no-match behavior.

target.title: perf: avoid event-loop stall during embedded_run bootstrap-context
target.body:
## Summary

Fix event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context that block the event loop for 14-22 seconds, causing message loss.

### Root causes addressed

1. **Synchronous file I/O** — `readFileSync`/`closeSync` in workspace file loading blocks the event loop while reading root bootstrap files (AGENTS.md, SOUL.md, etc.)
2. **Unbounded blocking glob walks** — `fs.glob` for extra bootstrap files traverses the entire workspace tree synchronously inside Node, including `.git`/`node_modules` directories, without yielding to the event loop
3. **Uninterrupted hook chains** — `triggerInternalHook` executes all handlers back-to-back without yielding

### Changes

**`src/agents/workspace.ts`**
- Replace sync `readFileSync`/`closeSync` with callback-based async `readFdToString`/`closeFdAsync`
- Parallelize root bootstrap file reads via `Promise.all` (previously serial `for` loop)
- Replace `fs.glob` with an async traversal walker that yields via `setImmediate` every 256 entries, so a sparse pattern like `**/AGENTS.md` over a large workspace never blocks the event loop. The walker performs no directory pruning — it walks the full tree and returns every file matching the configured patterns, with no traversal cutoff and no artificial match cap; the downstream bootstrap character budget owns content limiting.
- Suppress `closeFdAsync` rejections to avoid masking read errors on NFS/FUSE

**`src/hooks/internal-hooks.ts`**
- Add `yieldBetweenHandlers` option with `setImmediate` yield between hook handlers
- Add per-handler timing instrumentation with handler index reporting
- Log warnings for slow handlers exceeding 500ms threshold

**`src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.ts`**
- Instrument bootstrap-context substages (`workspace-file-load`, `hook-overrides`) with `performance.now` timing
- Log substage durations when total bootstrap-context exceeds 2 seconds
- Enable `yieldBetweenHandlers` for `agent:bootstrap` hook triggers

**`src/agents/bootstrap-files.ts`** / **`src/agents/bootstrap-hooks.ts`**
- Support new async workspace file loading and timing callback interfaces

### Tests

- `workspace.test.ts` — 3 new tests for async fd read (happy path, cache hit, fd close on failure)
- `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` — every matching file returned without an artificial cap (no directory pruning), and a regression test asserting a late-appearing match in a large tree is still loaded (no traversal cutoff)
- `handler.test.ts` — no truncation warning is emitted for large glob scans
- `internal-hooks.test.ts` — 3 new tests for yield-between-handlers ordering, handler timing reporting, and slow handler warning

All existing tests pass without modification.

### Performance impact

- Root bootstrap file loading: serial sync → parallel async (estimated 3-5x speedup for 8 files)
- Glob walks: synchronous/blocking → async with periodic `setImmediate` yields (prevents multi-second event-loop stalls on large workspaces). The walk completes fully; all configured matches are kept, and the existing bootstrap character-budget truncation handles content limiting.
- Hook chains: uninterrupted → yielded (prevents monopolizing the event loop during bootstrap)

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Event-loop stalls during `embedded_run` bootstrap-context loading caused by synchronous I/O and a blocking `fs.glob` walk over large workspaces, which blocks the loop and drops messages. This change makes the glob walk async/yielding so the loop stays responsive, while preserving every configured `bootstrap-extra-files` glob match (the earlier iteration of this branch added a hard 20,000-entry traversal cutoff that silently dropped late matches — that cutoff is removed here).
- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw checkout on macOS arm64, Node.js `v24.10.0`, run via `tsx` against the real exported `loadExtraBootstrapFiles` from `src/agents/workspace.ts`. Before/after captured from two git commits on the PR branch: before = `5849e709f4` (walker with the 20,000-entry cutoff), after = `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed).
- **Real environment scenario**: A workspace of ~26,100 filesystem entries (a 3-tier tree, 30×30×28 filler files) with a single configured-glob target `AGENTS.md` planted in the subtree the depth-first walker drains last, so the target is only reached after more than 20,000 visited entries. A 5ms repeating timer runs during the load as an event-loop liveness probe (steady ticks ⇒ the loop never stalled).
- **Failing proof before fix**: commit `5849e709f4` (cutoff present):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 82.9ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 16 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 0
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: false
  RESULT: late configured glob match was DROPPED (pre-fix behavior)
  ```
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```
  # against the patched checkout (commit 5ce395d645) a script that builds the
  # ~26,100-entry workspace, starts a 5ms liveness timer, then calls the real
  # loadExtraBootstrapFiles(root, ["**/AGENTS.md"]) and checks the deep match:
  npx tsx proof-live.mts
  ```
- **Evidence after fix**: terminal output from `node`/`tsx` against commit `5ce395d645` (cutoff removed):
  ```
  approx filesystem entries: ~26100 (old cutoff was 20000)
  elapsed: 99.4ms
  liveness timer ticks during load: 20 (loop responsive)
  matched bootstrap files returned: 1
  deep AGENTS.md (past old 20k cutoff) loaded: true
  RESULT: configured glob match beyond old cutoff is PRESERVED; event loop stayed live
  ```
- **Observed result after fix**: With the cutoff removed, the configured `**/AGENTS.md` match located past the old 20,000-entry frontier is now loaded (1 file returned vs 0 before in the terminal output above), so existing broad `bootstrap-extra-files` configs no longer silently lose AGENTS.md/TOOLS.md context. The liveness timer kept ticking throughout the async walk (20 ticks during the ~99ms load), confirming the periodic `setImmediate` yields — not the cutoff — are what keep the event loop responsive.
- **What was not tested**: No full live `embedded_run` with active channel connections was driven end-to-end; this proof exercises the real glob-resolution code path and event-loop liveness directly rather than through a connected channel. The focused regression coverage lives in `workspace.load-extra-bootstrap-files.test.ts` and `handler.test.ts`.

Closes #87509
target.author: jerry-xin

commands.author: wangwllu

target.title: fix: handle Claude CLI synthetic placeholders
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #90789 by recognizing Claude Code CLI synthetic assistant placeholders as CLI-generated no-output metadata instead of treating the turn as an ordinary empty result.

This covers the observed `model: "<synthetic>"` assistant events with:

- `No response requested.`
- `Continue from where you left off.`

## What changed

- Detect Claude stream-json assistant records where `message.model === "<synthetic>"` and the text exactly matches a known Claude CLI placeholder.
- Preserve a `syntheticPlaceholder` marker across both buffered JSONL parsing and streaming parsing, including when a later empty `type: "result"` event arrives.
- Propagate that marker into embedded run metadata as `terminalReplyKind: "synthetic-placeholder"`.
- **Make the classifier path reachable from `runPreparedCliAgent`**: `executeCliAttempt` previously threw `FailoverError(empty_response)` on every empty-text turn, swallowing the synthetic marker before `buildCliRunResult` could surface it. The throw is now skipped when `output.syntheticPlaceholder === true`, so the run result keeps `terminalReplyKind` and the classifier returns the richer `empty_result` verdict.
- Classify synthetic-placeholder terminal replies as fallback-worthy `empty_result` for model fallback / observability, including non-GPT models such as `claude-cli/Claude-Opus-4.7`.
- Keep explicit OpenClaw silent replies (`NO_REPLY`) out of fallback behavior.

## Rationale

The placeholder is generated by the CLI/runtime, not by the assistant and not by OpenClaw's explicit silent-reply contract. Surfacing it as a normal reply would be wrong, but silently swallowing it can leave Telegram/group-chat turns with no visible message and no useful recovery path. Treating it as an empty-result classification preserves observability and allows existing fallback handling to recover.

## Tests

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.context-engine.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`
  - 4 files passed, 50 tests passed
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/cli-output.ts src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts`
  - 0 warnings, 0 errors
- `pnpm exec oxfmt src/agents/cli-runner.ts src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`
  - clean
- `git diff --check`
  - clean

New `src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts` drives `runPreparedCliAgent` end-to-end with a mocked CLI output carrying `syntheticPlaceholder: true`, asserting both that `terminalReplyKind` reaches the result metadata and that `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback` emits the `empty_result` verdict — the integration coverage ClawSweeper P1 asked for.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Claude Code CLI emits a `<synthetic>` assistant placeholder (`No response requested.` / `Continue from where you left off.`) followed by an empty `type: "result"` event when its loop is wedged/timed-out. Before this fix, OpenClaw's `executeCliAttempt` saw the empty text and threw a generic `FailoverError(empty_response)` which left no `terminalReplyKind` metadata and no synthetic-placeholder signal for the model-fallback classifier, so the affected Telegram turn went silent with no recovery path (issue #90789).
- **Real environment tested**: local OpenClaw checkout at `/Users/wanglu241/code/openclaw`, branch `fix/90789-claude-cli-synthetic-placeholder` (head `8d6f1a7a`), node v26.0.0, pnpm/tsx 4.22.3, macOS 25.4.0 arm64. Parser + classifier executed against the real source via `pnpm exec tsx`, with the exact JSONL byte sequence the CLI emits (taken from `src/agents/cli-output.test.ts`'s captured fixture).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Build a one-off harness (`scratch/synthetic-placeholder-proof.ts`) that calls `parseCliJsonl(...)` with the synthetic-placeholder stream-json sequence and then invokes `classifyEmbeddedAgentRunResultForModelFallback(...)` against the resulting run-result shape.
  2. Check out the upstream/main versions of `src/agents/cli-output.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/result-fallback-classifier.ts`, and `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/types.ts`; run the harness; record output.
  3. Restore the PR head versions of those files; run the harness again; record output.
  4. Restore working tree, run `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts` to drive the same flow through `runPreparedCliAgent` end-to-end.
- **Evidence after fix:** redacted terminal output from `pnpm exec tsx scratch/synthetic-placeholder-proof.ts`:

```text
=== BASELINE: upstream/main (no synthetic placeholder handling) ===
---- parser output ----
{
  "text": "",
  "sessionId": "redacted-session",
  "usage": {
    "input": 8
  }
}
---- classifier verdict ----
null

=== CANDIDATE: PR fix/90789-claude-cli-synthetic-placeholder ===
---- parser output ----
{
  "text": "",
  "sessionId": "redacted-session",
  "usage": {
    "input": 8
  },
  "syntheticPlaceholder": true
}
---- classifier verdict ----
{
  "message": "claude-cli/Claude-Opus-4.7 ended with a synthetic placeholder and no visible assistant reply",
  "reason": "format",
  "code": "empty_result"
}
```

End-to-end `runPreparedCliAgent` integration test live output (`node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts`):

```text
 RUN  v4.1.7 /Users/wanglu241/code/openclaw

 ✓ |agents| src/agents/cli-runner.synthetic-placeholder.test.ts (2 tests) 3.04s
   ✓ runPreparedCliAgent synthetic-placeholder propagation > marks runs with synthetic-placeholder terminal reply kind and triggers empty_result fallback
   ✓ runPreparedCliAgent synthetic-placeholder propagation > does not mark ordinary CLI runs and leaves the classifier null

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  2 passed (2)
```

- **Observed result after fix**: with the PR applied, the JSONL parser surfaces `syntheticPlaceholder: true` (baseline does not), the run result carries `terminalReplyKind: "synthetic-placeholder"` (baseline strips it), and the model-fallback classifier returns `{ reason: "format", code: "empty_result", message: "...synthetic placeholder..." }` (baseline returns `null`). Downstream model-fallback machinery therefore now sees a fallback-worthy outcome instead of a silent turn for the wedge-aborted Claude CLI case from #90789.
- **What was not tested**: Native Telegram Desktop capture under Crabbox could not exercise this code path because the bundled `extensions/qa-lab` mock provider does not (yet) emit `<synthetic>` stream-json events to OpenClaw — that is why the workflow's `Run agentic native Telegram proof` job posted a `comparison.status: skipped, pass: false` capture-infrastructure manifest. Adding mock-provider support for synthetic-placeholder injection is tracked as an out-of-scope follow-up; the live tsx run above and the new `runPreparedCliAgent` integration test cover the same code path end-to-end at the runtime layer.

## Out-of-scope follow-ups

- Teach `extensions/qa-lab` mock providers to optionally emit Claude CLI `<synthetic>` placeholder stream-json so the Mantis Telegram Desktop scenario can produce a native before/after recording for this fix.
